conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Hello! I'd be happy to help you determine if you're eligible for Child Benefit. To assess your eligibility, I'll need to ask you some questions about your circumstances.

Let me first review the official eligibility rules, and then I'll ask you for the information I need.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for waiting. Now let me start by asking you some questions.

**Do you have any children you're enquiring about for Child Benefit?** If yes, how many children do you have, and could you tell me their names and ages?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text:
Hello! Yes, I'm interested in claiming Child Benefit and would like to know if I'm eligible.

I have one child - Alex, who is 21 years old.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for that information. I can see that Alex is 21 years old.

To help assess your eligibility, I need to ask: **Is Alex currently in approved education or training?**

(Child Benefit can normally only be claimed for children under 16, or under 20 if they're in approved education or training.)

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, Alex isn't in any education or training at the moment.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text: Thank you for providing that information. Let me also ask: **Do you live in the UK?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, I live in the UK - I'm in Manchester.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for confirming that. I now have enough information to assess your eligibility for Child Benefit for Alex.

Based on the official rules I've reviewed, I need to give you the outcome:

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
I'm sorry, but **you are not eligible for Child Benefit for Alex**.

Here's why: Child Benefit is available for children under 16, or for young people under 20 if they remain in approved education or training. Since Alex is 21 years old, they are above the maximum age threshold for Child Benefit, regardless of whether they are in education or training.

Child Benefit would have stopped when Alex either turned 20 (if they were in approved education or training) or when they turned 16 (if they were not in approved education or training).

If you have any other questions or if your circumstances change in the future, please feel free to ask.

claimant_lives_in_uk.description: Whether the claimant lives in the UK.

children.description: A list of children the claimant is claiming for.

lives_with_claimant.description: Whether the child lives with the claimant.

in_approved_education.description: Whether the child is in approved education or training (e.g. A-levels, GCSEs, NVQ up to level 3). Only relevant for children aged 16-19.

in_extension_period.description: Whether the child (aged 16 or 17) has left education and registered with a government-sponsored careers service or the armed forces (qualifying for a 20-week extension).

upkeep_per_week.description: The amount in £ per week the claimant contributes towards the child's upkeep (food, clothes, pocket money, etc.). Only relevant if the child does not live with the claimant.

another_claimant_has_priority.description: Whether another person who also lives with the child has priority for claiming Child Benefit.

another_claimant_lives_with_child.description: Whether someone who lives with the child is already claiming Child Benefit for them.

care_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in local authority care.

care_home_24h_per_week.description: Whether the child spends at least 24 hours per week at home while in local authority care.

hospital_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in hospital or residential accommodation.

claimant_spends_on_child.description: Whether the claimant regularly spends money on the child (e.g. food, clothes, books) while the child is in hospital or residential accommodation.

is_fostered.description: Whether the child is fostered by the claimant.

council_pays_for_child.description: Whether the local council pays towards the child's accommodation or maintenance.

works_24_plus_hours.description: Whether the child works 24 or more hours per week in paid employment.

started_apprenticeship_in_england.description: Whether the child has started an apprenticeship in England.

receives_qualifying_benefits.description: Whether the child receives qualifying benefits in their own right, such as Universal Credit or Employment and Support Allowance.

test_case.agent_script:
=== YOUR SITUATION PROFILE ===
You live in the UK.
You are inquiring about your 1 child:
  - Alex is 21 years old and does not live with you.

Here are the exact details of your circumstances to use when answering the agent's questions:

Regarding Alex:
  - Claimant lives in the UK
  - Child is 21, which is 20 or over
  - Child does not live with claimant and weekly upkeep (£20.00) is below the Child Benefit rate (£26.05)
  - Child is not in local authority care
  - Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
  - Child is not fostered
  - Child does not work 24+ hours/week
  - Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
  - Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

expected_eligibility.name: Alex
expected_eligibility.reason: Child is 21, which is 20 or over; Child does not live with claimant and weekly upkeep (£20.00) is below the Child Benefit rate (£26.05)
circumstances: Child is 21, which is 20 or over
Child does not live with claimant and weekly upkeep (£20.00) is below the Child Benefit rate (£26.05)
Child is not in local authority care
Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

reasonings: Alex is 21 years old and is not in approved education or training. According to the Child Benefit eligibility rules, you normally qualify for Child Benefit if you're responsible for a child under 16. If your child is 16 or over, you'll normally still qualify if your child is under 20 and they stay in approved education or training. Since Alex is 21 years old, they are above the maximum age threshold for Child Benefit eligibility, regardless of their education or training status. Therefore, you are not eligible for Child Benefit for Alex.
arguments.overall_reasoning: The claimant lives in the UK and has one child, Alex, who is 21 years old and not in education or training. Child Benefit is only available for children under 16, or under 20 if in approved education or training. As Alex is 21, they exceed the maximum age limit for Child Benefit eligibility.

If your child is 16 or over, you'll normally still qualify if your child is under 20 and they stay in approved education or training. Since Alex is 21 years old, they are above the maximum age threshold for Child Benefit eligibility, regardless of their education or training status. Therefore, you are not eligible for Child Benefit for Alex.

response.overall_reasoning: The claimant lives in the UK and has one child, Alex, who is 21 years old and not in education or training. Child Benefit is only available for children under 16, or under 20 if in approved education or training. As Alex is 21, they exceed the maximum age limit for Child Benefit eligibility.
